prompt:
You are a PROOF-FILLER. Replace the `sorry` placeholders for the listed declarations with complete, type-correct Lean proofs. Do NOT change statement signatures; do NOT weaken hypotheses.

Lean files live under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/FiniteDimL1LinfDuality
Target declarations: CausalSmith.Substrate.FiniteDimL1LinfDuality.exists_moment_le_dual, CausalSmith.Substrate.FiniteDimL1LinfDuality.sInf_primal_le_sSup_dual

## Instructions
Fill the 2 `sorry`s in /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/FiniteDimL1LinfDuality/StrongDuality.lean WITHOUT changing statements. Read that file (its docstring has the full 6-step Hahn–Banach roadmap), plus Basic.lean and WeakDuality.lean (you may use their lemmas even if still `sorry`). THIS IS THE HARD DIRECTION — do NOT weaken/special-case the statement to force a pass; if a genuine Mathlib gap blocks step 5, leave that single sub-goal as `sorry`, prove everything else, and add a comment stating the exact remaining goal + the missing lemma.

Goal `exists_moment_le_dual (hp : Function.Injective p) (hβ : β ≤ k) : ∃ w ∈ MomentSol p β, ∑ j, |w j| ≤ sSup (dualValSet p β)`. Strategy: work in `E := PiLp ∞ (fun _ : Fin (k+1) => ℝ)` (sup norm; dual pairs with ℓ¹). (1) Node-value subspace `V = Submodule.span ℝ {v | ∃ r, r.natDegree ≤ β ∧ v = fun j => r.eval (p j)}`. (2) Contrast functional `φ : V →ₗ ℝ`, `φ (node-values of r) = r.eval 1 - r.eval 0`; WELL-DEFINED because distinct nodes + deg ≤ β ≤ k make node-evaluation injective on degree-≤β polys (reuse the injectivity behind `momentSol_nonempty` / Causalean `nondegenerate_of_distinct_points`). (3) `‖φ‖ = sSup (dualValSet p β)` (positive homogeneity + attainment on the finite-dim unit ball). (4) Extend to `g : E →L[ℝ] ℝ` with `‖g‖ = ‖φ‖` via `exists_extension_norm_eq` (real Hahn–Banach; check exact name/signature with lean-lsp). (5) Represent `g` by `w j := g (EuclideanSpace.single j 1)` (or the `PiLp` basis vector); prove `∑ j, |w j| = ‖g‖` (ℓ∞ dual = ℓ¹ isometry — search `PiLp` dual / `PiLp.norm` lemmas) and `∀ b, g b = ∑ j, w j * b j`. (6) For each `ℓ ≤ β`, apply `g` to the node values of `X^ℓ`: `∑ j, w j * (p j)^ℓ = φ(...) = 1^ℓ - 0^ℓ`, so `w ∈ MomentSol p β`; and `∑ j, |w j| = ‖g‖ = ‖φ‖ = sSup dualValSet`. Then `sInf_primal_le_sSup_dual` follows: `csInf_le primalNormSet_bddBelow ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩` gives `sInf ≤ ∑|w j| ≤ sSup`.

Heavily use lean-lsp (lean_leansearch, lean_loogle, lean_hover_info, lean_goal, lean_multi_attempt) to find exact Mathlib names for: Hahn–Banach extension, PiLp ∞/1 norms and duality, EuclideanSpace.single, finite-dim sup attainment. After editing run `cd /<repo-root>/CausalSmith && lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.FiniteDimL1LinfDuality.StrongDuality` and report exactly which sorries (if any) remain and why. Do not modify other files.

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you closed, which remain, and any blocker. No JSON required.
